req.title: Submit timesheet — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a weekly timesheet submission with per-day hours [0,24] and weekly total [0,80], plus rate-limiting. I apply EP to cover a valid mid-range submission (positive), NEG/DT for a per-day value exceeding 24 (negative), BVA for exact boundary values (0 and 24 per day, total exactly 80), STC for the draft→submitted state transition, and NEG for the rate-limit pathway. Five tests cover the minimum three kinds required and exercise all specified error pathways.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_mid_range
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: Build a payload with 5 working days each having 8 hours and 2 rest days with 0 hours (total=40)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
trace: R-WEB-070: per-day and weekly totals within spec

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceed_24_returns_422
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: Build a payload where Monday has 25 hours (outside 0-24 range)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body references the per-day hours validation error
trace: R-WEB-070: error pathway — 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers_fixture>

def test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceed_24_returns_422(auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "week_start": "<current_week_monday>",
        "days": [
            {"day": "monday",    "hours": 25},
            {"day": "tuesday",   "hours": 8},
            {"day": "wednesday", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "thursday",  "hours": 8},
            {"day": "friday",    "hours": 8},
            {"day": "saturday",  "hours": 0},
            {"day": "sunday",    "hours": 0}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/submit",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert <field_error_present>(body, "hours")

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_max_day_and_max_weekly
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: Build a payload: Mon-Fri each 16 hours (total=80), Sat-Sun 0 hours; all days at or below per-day max of 24
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
trace: R-WEB-070: BVA upper boundary — weekly total exactly 80 accepted

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: Build a payload: Mon-Sun each 12 hours (total=84, exceeds 80) — each day individually valid (12<=24)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body references the weekly total validation error
trace: R-WEB-070: error pathway — 422 when weekly total outside 0-80
R-WEB-070: DT — per-day valid but weekly sum invalid triggers 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers_fixture>

def test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422(auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "week_start": "<current_week_monday>",
        "days": [
            {"day": "monday",    "hours": 12},
            {"day": "tuesday",   "hours": 12},
            {"day": "wednesday", "hours": 12},
            {"day": "thursday",  "hours": 12},
            {"day": "friday",    "hours": 12},
            {"day": "saturday",  "hours": 12},
            {"day": "sunday",    "hours": 12}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/submit",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert <field_error_present>(body, "total_hours")

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for the user is at or near the configured threshold
actions: Submit a valid timesheet N+1 times in rapid succession where N equals the per-user rate limit
Capture the response of the final (N+1th) request
expected: The first N requests return 200 or 422 (valid processing)
The (N+1)th request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response includes Retry-After header or rate-limit error body
trace: R-WEB-070: variant 4 semantics — rate-limited per user
R-WEB-070: STC — transition from allowed state to rate-limited state
